hooks.command:
cat "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || echo $PWD)/.github/copilot-instructions.md" 2>/dev/null | head -120 | awk '{gsub(/\/,"\\"); gsub(/"/,"\""); x=x $0 "\n"} END{printf "{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"%s"}}", x}' 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"MANDATORY: Read .github/copilot-instructions.md and CONVENTIONS.md before any task.

FOLDER STRUCTURE RULES:
- Source files (.ts, .tsx, .css) go directly in the feature folder.
- Test files (.test.ts, .test.tsx) go EXCLUSIVELY inside a tests/ subdirectory.
- NEVER place a test file at the same level as source files.
- CORRECT: src/tabs/home/basic-text/tests/BasicTextGroup.test.tsx
- WRONG:   src/tabs/home/basic-text/BasicTextGroup.test.tsx
- Every new logical block (pure function, hook, utility) gets its own file + tests/ subfolder with unit tests.

CODE QUALITY RULES:
1. Stack: React, RTL, Node, TypeScript only.
2. No globals - use React context, never window._anything.
3. No side effects at module load.
4. No vitest - Jest 30 + RTL only. Never import from vitest.
5. TypeScript strict mode - no any except mock-casting in test-utils.
6. Editor guard in every handler: const editor = getEditor(); if (!editor) return;
7. CSS: all classes prefixed onr-. No Tailwind, no utility classes.
8. Full variable names - no abbreviations. editor not ed, index not i.
9. Comments on non-obvious lines only.
10. Blank line between distinct logical sections.
11. Extract multi-line onClick/onMouseDown to named functions.
12. No console.log in production code.

PLAN EXECUTION:
1. Follow the plan exactly - no skipping or reordering.
2. SPAWN SUBAGENTS - one per plan section. Never execute full plan inline.
3. Always finish the task.
4. Do not mark complete until npm test passes and build is clean."}}'

printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"Verify the file you just wrote follows CONVENTIONS.md: §4 folder structure (test files in tests/ only), §5 React patterns (editor guard, named handlers, RibbonButton/GroupShell/Dropdown), §6 naming (onr- prefix, no abbreviations, full variable names), §7 CSS (onr- prefix, no Tailwind), §8 test conventions (tests/ subdirectory, RTL patterns, coverage), §10 code quality (no globals, no console.log, blank lines between sections, TypeScript strict, no vitest)."}}'
hooks.statusMessage: Reminding code quality and folder structure rules...
